Chapter 7
Structure and Preparation of Alkenes: Elimination Reactions

Section 7.1
ALKENE NOMENCLATURE
©2020 McGraw-Hill Education. All rights reserved. Authorized only for instructor use in the classroom. No reproduction or further distribution permitted without the prior written consent of McGraw-Hill Education .

Naming Alkenes
In the name of the corresponding alkane, - ane is replaced with – ene
The parent chain of the compound includes the double bond
The chain is numbered to give the doubly bound carbons the lowest numbers possible
Priorities of Substituents
Alkene-containing Groups

Cycloalkenes
Cycloalkenes are numbered such that the double bond connects C-1 and C-2
Number in the direction that gives the smaller numbers to substituents

Section 7.2
STRUCTURE AND BONDING IN ALKENES
The Structure of Ethylene

The Structure of Propylene
Propene (C 3 H 6 ) includes a C = C bond and a C – C bond
The C = C carbons are both sp 2 hybridized and trigonal planar; the –CH 3 carbon is sp 3 hybridized and tetrahedral

Section 7.3
ISOMERISM IN ALKENES

Alkene Isomers of C 4 H 8
There are four alkene isomers of C 4 H 8

cis - trans Isomerism
Substituents can be on the same side ( cis ) or opposite sides ( trans ) of a double bond
Not possible when one carbon of the double bond bears two identical substituents
Interconversion of cis and trans isomers via C = C rotation is extremely slow at normal temperatures

Section 7. 4
NAMING STEREOISOMERIC ALKENES BY THE E–Z NOTATIONAL SYSTEM

Extending cis - trans Isomerism
For 1,1-disubstituted alkenes, the assignment of cis or trans is straightforward
What about trisubstituted and tetrasubstituted alkenes? How do we know which groups to compare?

The E / Z System
Prioritize the two groups attached to each carbon relative to one another. Find the higher-priority group on each carbon
If the higher priority groups are cis , the geometry is Z ( zusammen , together)
If the higher priority groups are trans , the geometry is E ( entgegen , opposite )

Stereoisomerism and Alkenes
Alkenes containing chirality centers may have stereoisomers involving R / S at the chirality center and E / Z at the alkene

Section 7.5
PHYSICAL PROPERTIES OF ALKENES

Alkenes Resemble Alkanes
The physical properties of alkenes resemble those of alkanes:
Low boiling and melting points, especially for low-molecular-weight compounds
Small dipole moments (if any)
Alkenes substituted with heteroatoms do have significant dipole moments

Alkyl Groups Are Electron Donating
Alkyl groups appear to be electron donating or electron releasing on the basis of dipole moments
Resonance structures illustrate the hyperconjugative donation of electrons from an alkyl group

Section 7.6
RELATIVE STABILITIES OF ALKENES

Trends in Alkene Stability
Three factors influence the stability of alkenes:
Degree of substitution of C=C (an electronic effect)
van der Waals strain in the cis stereoisomer (a steric effect)
Chain branching
Substitution: additional alkyl groups bonded to C=C stabilize the double bond

Trends in Alkene Stability-1
Three factors influence the stability of alkenes:
Degree of substitution of C=C (an electronic effect)
van der Waals strain in the cis stereoisomer (a steric effect)
Chain branching
van der Waals strain/Steric crowding: cis isomers are destabilized by interactions between the close alkyl groups

Trends in Alkene Stability-2
Three factors influence the stability of alkenes:
Degree of substitution of C=C (an electronic effect)
van der Waals strain in the cis stereoisomer (a steric effect)
Chain branching
Chain branching: 1,1-disubstituted alkenes are more stable than cis and trans 1,2-disubstituted alkenes

Section 7.7
CYCLOALKENES

Cyclic Alkenes
Double bonds can be found in rings of all sizes. The smallest cycloalkene is cyclopropene
Cycloalkenes differ in conformation from their saturated analogues
Interconversion of these half-chair conformers occurs readily .

cis - trans Isomerism in Cycloalkenes
Small cycloalkenes universally contain cis double bonds
For cyclooctene and larger, the double bond may have the cis or the trans configuration!
The energy difference diminishes with increasing ring size
